list.title: Instances
list.subtitle: Create, connect, and manage every OpenClaw runtime that should participate in this workspace.

summary.openclaw: The primary built-in kernel supported directly by Agent Studio and ready for managed installation.
summary.hermes: A multi-kernel runtime that standardizes supported local-managed, local-external, and remote Linux deployment environments.

platforms.wsl2OrRemoteOnly: Linux / macOS / Windows WSL2 / Remote Linux

list.emptyDescription: Once instances are registered, this page will show their status, resource metrics, and detail entry points.
list.confirmUninstall: Are you sure you want to uninstall this instance?

onboarding.builtInDescription: Use the guided install flow to pick the best supported host method and keep the built-in OpenClaw runtime under Agent Studio control.
onboarding.associateDescription: Scan this machine for existing OpenClaw installs created by pnpm, npm, Docker, WSL, source workflows, and other supported methods.
onboarding.remoteDescription: Register an already running OpenClaw gateway over HTTP and WebSocket so the workspace can target it directly.

associateDialog.title: Associate installed OpenClaw
associateDialog.description: Link OpenClaw runtimes that already exist on this machine and keep the instance registry aligned with their current config.
associateDialog.detectedDescription: Each detected method is inspected against the local host and matched to an existing local-external instance when possible.
associateDialog.emptyTitle: No installed OpenClaw runtimes were detected
associateDialog.emptyDescription: If OpenClaw was installed manually or through an unsupported path, attach it directly from its openclaw.json file below.
associateDialog.manualDescription: Choose an existing openclaw.json file when the runtime was installed outside the guided installer or cannot be auto-discovered reliably.
associateDialog.manualDesktopOnly: Manual file association is only available in the desktop app.
associateDialog.configMissingHint: This install record exists, but its OpenClaw config file could not be located automatically. Use manual association to pick the config file directly.

remoteDialog.title: Create remote OpenClaw instance
remoteDialog.description: Register a remote OpenClaw gateway so it appears as a first-class instance inside Agent Studio.

fields.name: Instance name
fields.secureDescription: Turn this on for HTTPS and secure WebSocket endpoints.

placeholders.name: Team Gateway
placeholders.authToken: Optional token used by the remote gateway
placeholders.description: Shared team OpenClaw runtime for staging or production traffic.

toasts.discoveryFailed: Unable to scan installed OpenClaw runtimes
toasts.configMissing: OpenClaw config file was not found for this install
toasts.associationFailed: Unable to associate the selected OpenClaw runtime
toasts.remoteCreateFailed: Unable to create the remote OpenClaw instance
toasts.openDiagnosticFailed: Unable to open the diagnostic location

nodes.description: Cluster governance starts here. Local primary kernels are listed beside attached or managed nodes so rollout, diagnostics, and topology drift can converge on one product model.

toasts.ensureSuccess: Local kernel node checked and attached.
toasts.ensureFailed: Failed to ensure the local kernel node is running.
toasts.restartFailed: Failed to restart the local kernel node.

metrics.totalNodesDetail: All local, managed, and attached kernels visible to the control plane.
metrics.managedDetail: Nodes where Agent Studio owns lifecycle and recovery decisions.
metrics.attachedDetail: Nodes that stay visible even when lifecycle ownership is partial.
metrics.needsAttentionDetail: Degraded or quarantined nodes that need intervention.

management.managedDescription: Managed nodes stay under Agent Studio lifecycle control.
management.attachedDescription: Attached nodes remain observable even when lifecycle stays elsewhere.

values.activeHostPlatform: The active host platform backing this node inventory.
values.internalSessionsReported: Internal node sessions currently reported by the host platform.

values.activeHostPlatform: The active host platform backing this node inventory.
values.internalSessionsReported: Internal node sessions currently reported by the host platform.

detail.dangerDescription: Irreversible actions for this instance. Proceed with caution.

fields.gatewayPortDescription: The port the Agent Studio daemon listens on.
fields.corsOriginsDescription: Allowed origins for API requests (comma separated).
fields.apiTokenDescription: Use this token to authenticate external applications with this gateway.
fields.agentSandboxDescription: Isolate skill execution in a secure sandbox environment.
fields.autoUpdateDescription: Automatically download and install daemon updates.

toasts.failedToRetryBundledStartup: Failed to retry built-in OpenClaw startup
toasts.failedToOpenDiagnosticPath: Failed to open the diagnostic location
toasts.failedToOpenControlPage: Failed to open the control page

detail.confirmUninstall: Are you sure you want to uninstall this instance? This action cannot be undone.

hermes.description: Hermes runs through standardized supported host environments. This detail module tracks transport posture, runtime policy, user-root kernel state, and the machine-facing evidence required to keep Hermes support predictable across supported local-managed, local-external, WSL2, and remote Linux deployments.

windows.title: Windows uses WSL2 or remote Linux
windows.detail: Native Windows local Hermes execution is out of scope. Windows hosts must attach Hermes through WSL2 or a remote Linux machine.

python.title: Python and uv stay external
python.detail: The desktop package never bundles Python or uv. Doctor and deployment flows must rely on externally installed runtimes.

node.title: Node.js stays external
node.detail: Node.js is not bundled. Hermes may use it for capability-scoped workflows, but installation and upgrades remain external.

deploymentTarget.label: Deployment target
deploymentTarget.configured: This instance already reports a supported Hermes deployment posture. Hermes may run as a local-managed or local-external runtime on Linux, macOS, or Windows WSL2, or as a remote Linux deployment.
deploymentTarget.required: Use a supported Hermes deployment posture: local-managed or local-external on Linux, macOS, or Windows WSL2, or a remote Linux deployment. Native Windows local execution remains unsupported.

endpointExposure.label: Connectivity endpoints
endpointExposure.configured: The shared base detail already exposes at least one published Hermes endpoint for operator access.
endpointExposure.required: No Hermes endpoint has been published yet. Keep endpoint verification in the Hermes module boundary until connectivity is visible.

observability.label: Observability evidence
observability.configured: Shared observability data is available for this instance, so runtime evidence can be reviewed without leaving the detail module.
observability.optional: No log evidence is exposed yet. Hermes can still run, but diagnostics remain limited until observability is surfaced.

hermes.emptyEndpoints: This Hermes instance has not published any connectivity endpoints yet.
hermes.emptyNotes: No additional Hermes notes are available for this instance yet.

unsupported.description: No registered detail module is available for {{kernel}} yet. This route now requires each kernel to provide its own detail implementation instead of reusing the OpenClaw page.

sidebar.title: Instance Workbench
sidebar.description: Switch between the runtime capabilities available on this OpenClaw instance.

itemDescriptions.overview: Runtime identity, connectivity, storage, and diagnostics
itemDescriptions.llmProviders: Provider routing, models, and inference controls
itemDescriptions.config: openclaw.json defaults, grouped controls, and raw file view

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Inspect the runtime contract, connectivity, storage binding, and capability posture for this instance.

channels.title: Chat Channels
channels.description: Inspect delivery readiness, configuration depth, and connection state for this instance.

cronTasks.title: Cron Tasks
cronTasks.description: Monitor scheduled jobs, recent runs, and the operational rhythm of this runtime.
cronTasks.editorNotice: OpenClaw runtime actions are live here, but task creation and editing still route through the legacy task editor. Use clone, run, pause, resume, delete, and history from this panel for now.
cronTasks.readonlyNotice: OpenClaw cron jobs are currently exposed as a read-only runtime snapshot. Create, edit, run, pause, and delete actions will be enabled after native runtime commands are wired.

llmProviders.title: LLM Providers
llmProviders.description: Inspect active providers, model routing, and the generation parameters shaping this instance.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Review the operator catalog and the focus areas available inside this instance.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: See which reusable skills are installed and ready to amplify local workflows.

files.title: Files
files.description: Manage gateway profile details and inspect the runtime files that support this instance.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: Audit retained runbooks, facts, and rolling context used by the runtime.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Inspect callable tools, execution privileges, and high-impact runtime controls.

config.title: Config
config.description: Inspect the attached openclaw.json, grouped defaults, and config-backed OpenClaw controls.

hero.title: openclaw.json Workbench
hero.description: Review the attached OpenClaw config the same way Control UI thinks about it: structured summaries first, raw document when you need the source of truth.

overview.groupsDescription: Top-level config keys are bucketed into the same domain model Control UI uses so you can scan the file before dropping into raw editing.

providers.sectionsDescription: These top-level sections shape model routing, agents, skills, and other AI-facing defaults inside openclaw.json.

tools.sectionsDescription: Communication-facing sections from openclaw.json are surfaced here so delivery routes and message surfaces stay easy to audit.

runtime.sectionsDescription: Core, automation, infrastructure, appearance, and custom top-level sections are grouped here as runtime-facing configuration.

raw.title: Raw openclaw.json
raw.invalidDraft: The current draft is not valid JSON5
raw.readonlyNotice: This config attachment is currently read-only, so the raw editor stays in preview mode.
raw.unsaved: You have unsaved raw config changes.

empty.channels: No channels are defined in the attached openclaw.json.
empty.providers: No providers are defined in the attached openclaw.json.
empty.agents: No agents are defined in the attached openclaw.json.
empty.aiSections: No AI-facing top-level sections were detected in the current openclaw.json draft.
empty.communicationSections: No communication-facing top-level sections were detected in the current openclaw.json draft.
empty.runtimeSections: No runtime-facing top-level sections were detected in the current openclaw.json draft.

schemaEditor.unsupportedArraySchema: Unsupported array schema. Use Raw mode.
schemaEditor.noItemsYet: No items yet. Add one to begin editing.
schemaEditor.noArrayItemsMatchSearch: No array items match the current search.
schemaEditor.customEntriesDescription: Keys outside the fixed schema live here.
schemaEditor.noCustomEntriesMatchSearch: No custom entries match the current search.
schemaEditor.noConfiguredFieldsYet: No configured fields in this object yet.
schemaEditor.saferInRawMode: This schema shape is safer to edit in Raw mode.
schemaEditor.schemaUnavailable: Config schema is unavailable for this section.
schemaEditor.invalidDraftForConfigMode: The current draft must be valid JSON5 before Config mode can edit it.
schemaEditor.dynamicPathsUseRawMode: Some schema paths are too dynamic for the structured editor and are safer to edit in Raw mode.
schemaEditor.hiddenSensitiveValues: {{count}} sensitive values are hidden in this section. Use reveal controls to inspect them.
schemaEditor.noSettingsMatchSearch: No settings in this section match the current search.
schemaEditor.noSettingsAvailable: No settings are available for this section.

overview.title: OpenClaw config overview
overview.description: Review section groups and jump into a specific area before editing.
overview.invalidDraft: Structured editing is paused until Raw fixes the invalid JSON5 draft.
overview.rawOnlyPaths: {{count}} schema paths remain raw-only, so some advanced settings are safer to edit in Raw mode.
overview.startWithSection: Start with {{section}} to edit this group.
overview.empty: No config groups are available yet.

navigation.invalidDraft: The current draft is invalid JSON5.

diff.title: Pending changes
diff.moreChanges: More changes remain in this draft.

raw.label: Raw config (JSON/JSON5)
raw.hiddenTitle: Hidden until you reveal sensitive values.
raw.hiddenDescription: Use reveal to inspect and edit the raw config.

errors.startUpdate: Failed to start the OpenClaw update.

empty.sectionsForInstance: No config sections are available for this instance.

descriptions.env: Environment variables passed to the gateway process
descriptions.acp: Agent Communication Protocol runtime and streaming settings
descriptions.__appearance__: Theme, color, and visual appearance configuration
descriptions.custom: Custom top-level section detected in openclaw.json.

webSearch.title: Web Search Configuration
webSearch.description: Manage the shared OpenClaw web-search runtime settings and the provider-specific plugin config that powers search results.
webSearch.enabledDescription: Toggle the shared web-search capability for this instance.
webSearch.advancedDescription: Advanced JSON is merged under the selected provider's canonical OpenClaw plugin webSearch config root.

errors.maxResultsInvalid: Max results must be a positive number.
errors.timeoutInvalid: Timeout must be a positive number.
errors.cacheTtlInvalid: Cache TTL must be a positive number.

toasts.saveFailed: Failed to save web search configuration.

webFetch.title: Web Fetch Configuration
webFetch.description: Manage the shared OpenClaw web_fetch runtime settings and the Firecrawl fallback config used when readable extraction needs provider-assisted fetch recovery.
webFetch.enabledDescription: Toggle the shared web_fetch capability for this instance.
webFetch.readabilityDescription: Use Readability extraction before falling back to Firecrawl.
webFetch.advancedDescription: Advanced JSON is merged under plugins.entries.firecrawl.config.webFetch after the API key and base URL fields are applied.

placeholders.userAgent: Leave blank to use the OpenClaw runtime default

errors.maxCharsInvalid: Max chars must be a positive number.
errors.maxCharsCapInvalid: Max chars cap must be a positive number.
errors.maxResponseBytesInvalid: Max response bytes must be a positive number.
errors.timeoutInvalid: Timeout must be a positive number.
errors.cacheTtlInvalid: Cache TTL must be a positive number.
errors.maxRedirectsInvalid: Max redirects must be a positive number.

toasts.saveFailed: Failed to save web fetch configuration.

xSearch.title: X Search Configuration
xSearch.description: Control the first-class xAI x_search tool and its shared XAI API key wiring without dropping back to raw openclaw.json edits.
xSearch.enabledDescription: Toggle the bundled x_search tool for this instance.
xSearch.inlineCitationsDescription: Return inline citations in x_search responses when the provider supports them.
xSearch.advancedDescription: Advanced JSON is merged under plugins.entries.xai.config.xSearch after the managed fields are applied. The API key is stored under the shared xAI webSearch auth root.

errors.maxTurnsInvalid: Max turns must be a positive number.
errors.timeoutInvalid: Timeout must be a positive number.
errors.cacheTtlInvalid: Cache TTL must be a positive number.

webSearchNativeCodex.title: OpenAI Codex Native Search
webSearchNativeCodex.description: Manage tools.web.search.openaiCodex so Codex-capable models can switch onto native provider search while managed web_search remains available for non-Codex paths.
webSearchNativeCodex.enabledDescription: Enable the provider-native Codex web_search bridge for Codex-capable models.
webSearchNativeCodex.advancedDescription: Advanced JSON is merged under tools.web.search.openaiCodex after the managed fields are applied.

toasts.saveFailed: Failed to save native Codex search configuration.

authCooldowns.title: Auth Cooldown Controls
authCooldowns.description: Inspect and adjust the OpenClaw auth cooldown settings used for rate limits, overload backoff, and billing retries.
authCooldowns.note: Leave a field blank to keep the upstream OpenClaw default for that cooldown.

errors.rateLimitedProfileRotationsInvalid: Rate-limited profile rotations must be a non-negative whole number.
errors.overloadedProfileRotationsInvalid: Overloaded profile rotations must be a non-negative whole number.
errors.overloadedBackoffMsInvalid: Overload backoff must be a non-negative whole number.
errors.billingBackoffHoursInvalid: Billing backoff must be a non-negative whole number.
errors.billingMaxHoursInvalid: Billing max wait must be a non-negative whole number.
errors.failureWindowHoursInvalid: Failure window must be a non-negative whole number.

toasts.saveFailed: Failed to save auth cooldown settings.

dreaming.title: Dreaming & Dream Diary
dreaming.description: Manage plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming and inspect whether the runtime is writing Dream Diary entries back into the memory surface.
dreaming.enabledDescription: Toggle OpenClaw dreaming for this instance.
dreaming.note: Leave cadence blank to keep the upstream OpenClaw dreaming schedule.

overview.connectivityDescription: Known transport surfaces and auth posture for this instance.
overview.noConnectivity: No connectivity metadata is available for this instance yet.
overview.capabilitiesDescription: See which runtime capabilities are ready, degraded, or still require integration work.
overview.dataAccessDescription: Understand how Agent Studio links to each runtime surface and whether that route is authoritative.
overview.noDataAccess: No data access routes are available for this instance yet.
overview.artifactsDescription: Inspect the concrete files, directories, endpoints, and storage bindings associated with this instance.
overview.noArtifacts: No concrete artifacts are available for this instance yet.

management.title: Management Surface
management.description: See who controls this instance, where the authoritative config lives, and how much of the runtime Agent Studio can manage directly.

bundledStartupFailure.title: Built-in OpenClaw Startup Failed
bundledStartupFailure.description: The desktop shell is available, but the last built-in OpenClaw startup attempt did not complete. Review the failure reason below before retrying or debugging the managed runtime.

actions.gatewayReadinessTimeout: The desktop shell is already running, but the bundled gateway never started accepting connections within the readiness window. Read the gateway log first, then the desktop main log, to see whether boot is still progressing, stalled during provider initialization, or blocked by another local service before retrying.
actions.runtimeAccessDenied: Windows denied access while Agent Studio was finalizing the built-in runtime files. Read the desktop main log and gateway log, then close stale OpenClaw or Agent Studio processes and release antivirus or indexer locks before retrying.
actions.checkLogsAndRetry: Read the gateway log and desktop main log to identify the last activation stage and supervisor error before retrying the built-in runtime.

lastActivationStage.label: Last Activation Stage
lastActivationStage.description: The final built-in OpenClaw startup stage recorded by the desktop shell before the current failure or stall.

gatewayLogPath.label: Gateway Log
gatewayLogPath.description: Inspect the built-in OpenClaw gateway log for boot output, provider initialization, and bind or listen failures.

desktopMainLogPath.label: Desktop Main Log
desktopMainLogPath.description: Inspect the desktop shell log for activation stages, supervisor retries, and runtime install or finalize errors.

details.controlPlane: The lifecycle owner and deployment posture determine whether this instance is bundled, attached from outside, or remotely administered.
details.installMethod: This reflects the install path detected for the linked OpenClaw control surface.
details.installMethodUnknown: No console install method was reported for this instance yet.
details.configManagedFile: Agent Studio discovered a writable OpenClaw config file and uses it as the authoritative source for instance configuration.
details.configManagedDirectory: Configuration is resolved from the kernel config directory binding instead of a single file path.
details.configRemoteEndpoint: Configuration is only exposed through a runtime endpoint, so management stays partial and depends on gateway support.
details.configMetadataOnly: Only metadata-level config discovery is available for this instance right now.
details.configUnavailable: No authoritative OpenClaw config file is currently attached to this instance.
details.workspaceAgent: This workspace comes from the current default agent and is the primary filesystem scope for prompts, skills, and agent files.
details.workspaceArtifact: This workspace path was discovered from the instance artifact map rather than the agent registry.
details.workspaceNamespace: No concrete workspace path is attached, so the storage namespace is shown as the fallback scope.
details.scopeFull: Provider routes stay read-only here and are managed through Provider Center, while agent CRUD, channel config, and file editing still route through the discovered OpenClaw config surface.
details.scopePartial: Chat, file, or model operations may still work, but full agent and channel management is not available without a writable config attachment.
details.scopeReadonly: This instance is currently discoverable, but Agent Studio should treat it as read-only until a writable management path is attached.

files.gatewayProfileDescription: Tune the gateway contract that powers this instance's runtime behavior and security posture.
files.runtimeArtifactsDescription: Configuration, prompts, logs, datasets, and generated artifacts bound to this instance.
files.selectFile: Select a file from the explorer to preview or edit it.
files.readonlyNotice: This artifact is currently exposed in preview mode to avoid drifting from the built-in OpenClaw runtime state.

llmProviders.streamingDescription: Enable token streaming for runtime responses and live previews.
llmProviders.requestOverridesDescription: Edit the official models.providers.<provider>.request transport override surface as JSON5.
llmProviders.requestOverridesHint: Supported keys: headers, auth, proxy, and tls.
llmProviders.requestOverridesInvalid: Request overrides must be valid JSON5.
llmProviders.readonlyNotice: Provider routes for this OpenClaw instance are managed through Provider Center. Use Provider Center to change the projected base URL, API key, or model routing.
llmProviders.selectProvider: Select an LLM provider to inspect or edit its runtime configuration.

toasts.modelsRequired: Add at least one model. Use one line per model: model-id=Display Name
toasts.defaultModelMissing: Default model must exist in the provider model list.
toasts.reasoningModelMissing: Reasoning model must exist in the provider model list.
toasts.embeddingModelMissing: Embedding model must exist in the provider model list.

panel.description: Provider and model CRUD write back to the real config-backed OpenClaw config and keep defaults plus agent model references synchronized.
panel.emptyDescription: Create the first provider to unlock real instance-scoped model routing.
panel.providerModelsDescription: Add, rename, or remove models from the selected provider catalog.
panel.selectProvider: Select a provider to manage its model catalog.

dialog.description: This writes a provider into the config-backed OpenClaw config for the current instance.

modelDialog.description: Model CRUD stays synchronized with OpenClaw default and agent model references.

deleteProviderDialog.title: Delete OpenClaw Provider
deleteProviderDialog.descriptionSuffix: from the config-backed OpenClaw config. Default and agent references will be cleaned automatically.

deleteModelDialog.title: Delete Provider Model
deleteModelDialog.descriptionSuffix: from the selected provider and prune stale OpenClaw references automatically.

agents.marketReadonlyNotice: This OpenClaw host does not currently expose a writable config file. Agent Market installs and agent CRUD stay disabled until the host surfaces a config-backed runtime route.

toasts.skillInstalled: OpenClaw skill installed into the default workspace.
toasts.skillInstallFailed: Failed to install the selected OpenClaw skill.
toasts.skillUpdateFailed: Failed to update the selected OpenClaw skill.
toasts.skillRemoveFailed: Failed to remove the selected OpenClaw workspace skill.

panel.descriptionPrefix: Agent identity, workspace, per-agent model overrides, and state directories are written back to the real config-backed OpenClaw config. Credentials still live inside each agent's
panel.descriptionSuffix: and are intentionally not shared automatically.
panel.creator: Creator
panel.workspaceInstallDescription: OpenClaw loads agent-specific skills from the selected workspace. Run the native OpenClaw CLI in this workspace to install a skill for the selected agent.
panel.defaultWorkspaceOnlyNotice: Direct install is only available while the default agent is selected because the upstream skills.install gateway action still targets the default OpenClaw workspace.

dialog.description: Agent IDs should stay stable. New agent credentials must be configured in the agent's own auth store after creation if the chosen provider requires per-agent auth.
dialog.defaultAgentDescription: OpenClaw routes the implicit main session to the first default agent.
dialog.streamingDescription: Stored as a per-agent model param override when disabled.

placeholders.workspace: Leave blank to let OpenClaw derive workspace-<agentId>

menu.title: Create Agent
menu.description: Choose how to create the next agent for this instance without leaving the current workbench.

custom.title: Start from scratch
custom.description: Define a new agent manually for this writable kernel instance.

library.title: Use local library
library.description: Browse reusable local kernel agents and use one as the starting template.

copy.title: Copy my agents
copy.description: Duplicate an agent that already belongs to this instance so you can iterate safely.

market.title: Install from market
market.description: Browse curated agent packages and install one directly into this instance.

library.title: Use local library
library.description: Browse reusable local kernel agents and use one as the starting template.

copy.title: Copy my agents
copy.description: Reuse an existing agent from this instance as the starting point for a new copy.

form.title: Create Agent
form.description: Create a new agent for the current instance and kernel.
form.copyDescription: Start from an existing local kernel agent, keep the portable settings, and create a new agent in this instance.

market.title: Install from market
market.description: Browse packaged agent templates and install the selected one directly into this instance.

status.emptyDescription: Create or import an agent into a writable kernel instance and it will appear here as a template.

deleteDialog.title: Delete OpenClaw Agent
deleteDialog.description: This removes the agent entry from the config-backed OpenClaw config. The workspace and agentDir files are not deleted automatically.

empty.llmProviders: No LLM providers are configured for this instance yet.

empty.skills: No skills are installed on this instance yet.
